results.title: Mamba-3 and State Space Models on GPU Cloud
results.content:
In 2026, SSMs moved from research curiosity to practical production option. The Mamba-3 release from CMU, Princeton, Cartesia AI, and Together AI in March 2026, along with AI21 Labs' Jamba hybrids and other SSM variants, gave teams access to models that handle 64K-128K contexts at a fraction of the GPU cost of equivalent transformers. For background on what KV cache pressure looks like in practice, see the KV cache optimization guide.

## Mamba-3 Architecture: How Linear-Time Inference Changes GPU Economics

At the architecture level, Mamba-3 replaces the multi-head attention (MHA) and KV cache mechanism with a selective state space layer. Each layer maintains a state matrix that represents compressed information from all prior tokens. On each new token, the model: [...] State space models (SSMs) like Mamba-3, released by researchers from CMU, Princeton, Cartesia AI, and Together AI in March 2026, offer a different architecture tradeoff: linear-time inference that gets cheaper as context length grows rather than more expensive. For workloads with long context windows, the GPU economics flip. A model that needs 6x the compute of a transformer at 2K context may need half the compute at 64K.

Another class of non-autoregressive models gaining traction in 2026 is diffusion language models. Deploying dLLMs on GPU cloud covers LLaDA 2 and Mercury, which use iterative denoising rather than recurrent state for parallel token generation. [...] For xLSTM and RWKV-7, the two other major linear-attention releases of 2026, see the xLSTM and RWKV-7 GPU cloud deployment guide. For Liquid AI's LFM family, which uses structured linear operators rather than SSM-style state spaces, see the LFM2-8B-A1B and LFM2-2.6B GPU cloud deployment guide. Another 2026 architecture worth tracking is log-linear attention (O(N log N)), which sits between Mamba-3's linear O(N) scaling and quadratic attention - the O(N log N) attention guide for GPU cloud covers the decision matrix and cost modeling.

results.title: Mamba-3: Improved Sequence Modeling using State Space Principles (Mar 2026)
results.content:
Summary:
Mamba-3 introduces three methodological improvements to the Mamba architecture: exponential-trapezoidal discretization for richer state dynamics, complex-valued state updates for better state tracking, and a Multi-Input Multi-Output (MIMO) formulation to improve hardware utilization during decoding. These innovations allow Mamba-3 to achieve a superior performance-efficiency Pareto frontier, outperforming Mamba-2 and Gated DeltaNet (GDN) on downstream language modeling and retrieval tasks while maintaining hardware-efficient constant memory requirements.

Key Topics:
- Mamba-3
- State Space Models (SSMs)
- Exponential-Trapezoidal Discretization
- MIMO SSM
- Complex-valued SSM
- Inference Efficiency
- State Tracking
- Linear-Time Sequence Mixers [...] just a stepping stone? Will the future be entirely hybrid? Well, hybrids offer the best of both worlds right now. Fast generation refreshed by perfect recall. But, pure models like Mamba 3 prove that looking at sequence modeling strictly through a state space perspective unlocks deeply mathematical optimizations. Like the exponential trapezoidal math acting as a convolution or complex data-dependent RoPE tracking logic. Exactly. You don't discover those if you only think in terms of keys and queries. It's a completely different paradigm. But, you know, stepping back from the paper for a second, the thing that sticks with me is the brutal pragmatism of the Mamba architecture. Bending the algorithms to fit the hardware. Right. We literally added mathematical complexity specifically because [...] Mamba 3, improved sequence modeling using state space principles. That is the paper today. Huge paper. Yeah. And look, if you are an AI researcher or an ML engineer, you already know inference time compute is your ultimate bottleneck. I mean, transformer scale terribly during generation because of that KV cache. Right, the memory just explodes. Exactly. But the current linear models like uh Mamba 2 and gated DeltaNet, they trade away way too much quality and basically fail at basic state tracking tasks. Which is a huge problem for actual deployment. Yeah. So, Mamba 3 solves this by introducing three massive upgrades. First, an exponential trapezoidal discretization that is totally upgrades the math. Second, a complex valued state update utilizing this data dependent RoPE trick to unlock

results.title: Mamba-3 vs Transformers: SSM Inference Speed 2026
results.content:
The Mamba-3 paper’s inclusion of RoPE makes this hybrid vision much easier to implement. Before Mamba-3, integrating SSM and attention layers meant dealing with incompatible position encoding schemes. Now both layer types can use the same RoPE embeddings, which simplifies the architecture and reduces engineering complexity.

One Reddit user on r/LocalLLaMA asked the question on everyone’s mind: “Has any lab started using SSMs? It seems like all the frontier models are still 100% transformer based.” The answer is that frontier models are quietly going hybrid. The pure Transformer era is ending, but the replacement isn’t pure SSM either. It’s a blend. [...] That just changed. On March 17, 2026, Albert Gu and his team at CMU and Princeton (with Tri Dao from Together AI contributing) released Mamba-3, an open-source state space model under Apache 2.0 that outperforms Transformer baselines by nearly 4% on language modeling benchmarks while running 7x faster on long-sequence inference tasks. The ICLR 2026 paper landed with a thud that echoed across ML Twitter within hours.

Anyone following the SSM space since Mamba-1 would admit it: few expected the gap to close this fast. The “will something replace Transformers?” debate is over. Now we’re arguing about timelines and transition paths.

## What state space models actually do differently [...] Sebastian Raschka summed up the practical reality well:

“Oh wow, Mamba-3 is here! For me, the most interesting use case of Mamba and Mamba-likes are the recent transformer attention hybrid architectures (Qwen3.5, Kimi Linear, etc.) Would be interesting to swap Gated DeltaNet with Mamba-3 (which now also has RoPE) in next gen hybrids.”
— @rasbt, March 18, 2026

## Hybrid architectures: the actual future

OK so I skipped over something important. Forget “SSMs replace Transformers.” Hybrid architectures that combine both are where this is actually headed.

Image 1

Mamba-3 state space model for inference efficiency

Summarized by AI from the post below



"Mamba-3 is a new state space model (SSM) designed with inference efficiency as the primary goal — a departure from Mamba-2, which optimized for training speed. The key upgrades are a more expressive recurrence formula, complex-valued state tracking, and a MIMO (multi-input, multi-output) variant that boosts accuracy without slowing down decoding.

The result: Mamba-3 SISO beats Mamba-2, Gated DeltaNet, and even Llama-3.2-1B (Transformer) on prefill+decode latency across all sequence lengths at the 1.5B scale.

The team also open-sourced the kernels, built using a mix of Triton, TileLang, and CuTe DSL for maximum hardware performance.

results.title: Medium
results.content:
Then a snake showed up.

In March 2026, a new model named Mamba-3 was released under the Apache 2.0 license. It beat the previous language modeling baseline for the Transformer (the most commonly used model) by almost 4%, and it outperformed the Transformer when run on very long sequences of text (up to 7 times faster). The Mamba paper was accepted for inclusion in ICLR 2026 (one of the most competitive Machine Learning conferences in the world).

And almost nobody freaked out. [...] Sitemap

# Mamba Unboxed: The State Space Model That’s Quietly Replacing Attention

## Attention is not all you need. Turns out, sometimes you need a snake.

Harish K

Harish K

17 min read

·

Apr 16, 2026

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Image by Author

In 2017, a paper titled “Attention Is All You Need” was released by a team of researchers at Google, which introduced the Transformer architecture. It changed everything in the deep learning landscape. For the next seven years, the title of this paper (and the Transformer architecture itself) would be held up as “gospel.”

Then a snake showed up.

results.title: Post-Transformer Architectures - Mamba and Hybrid Models
results.content:
# Typical output:
#  seq_len  Attention  SSM  ratio
# --------------------------------------------------
#  512  0.0023s  0.0018s  1.28×
#  1024  0.0045s  0.0032s  1.41×
#  2048  0.0156s  0.0061s  2.56×
#  4096  0.0589s  0.0118s  4.99×
#  8192  0.2234s  0.0233s  9.58×`

The SSM advantage grows as sequence length increases. At 8K tokens, SSM is ~10× faster. At 128K tokens, the advantage is measured in orders of magnitude.

### 32.8.5 Academic vs Industrial Perspective [...] The field moves fast. These chapters represent a snapshot of April 2026. What will not move: the first-principles thinking that makes new papers comprehensible. The math and the intuition you built in Parts 1–8 are the durable part. Everything in Part 9 is the application.

`Zhang, Wayland (2026). Chapter 32: Post-Transformer Architectures - Mamba and Hybrid Models. In Transformer Architecture: From Intuition to Implementation. 

© 2026 Wayland Zhang · MIT Licensed

My social channels:

© 2026 MIT Licensed [...] ## 32.2 State Space Models

### 32.2.1 RNN Context

Before Transformers, sequence modeling used RNNs. Their core idea: compress the entire history into a fixed-size hidden state.

`h_t = f(h_{t-1}, x_t)  # update state
y_t = g(h_t)  # produce output`

RNN advantages: O(N) compute, O(1) inference memory (just the hidden state).

RNN disadvantages: sequential computation (cannot parallelize), gradient vanishing/exploding (hard to capture long-range dependencies), and a fixed-size bottleneck that cannot hold all relevant context.

Transformers solved the quality problem but reintroduced the O(N²) scaling problem. SSMs try to get O(N) scaling with better quality than RNNs.

### 32.2.2 The State Space Equations

results.title: Mamba-3, Explained Simply: Why State Space Models Are Quietly Catching Up to Transformers | by Varun Nathan | Towards AI
results.content:
·

Apr 11, 2026

--

> Paper
>
> Code

## TL;DR

Mamba-3 is a new sequence model that tries to keep the efficiency promise of state space models without giving up too much quality. Its three big ideas are: a better way to convert continuous dynamics into token-by-token updates, a complex-valued state that helps with tracking structured patterns over long sequences, and a MIMO formulation that improves modeling power without increasing decode-time state size. The result is a stronger performance-efficiency tradeoff than earlier Mamba-style models, with gains on language modeling, retrieval, state-tracking, and long-context extrapolation.

## Why this paper matters
